taskDescription: Click on the key special senses terms in the text below.
textField:
The *retina* contains two types of *photoreceptors*: *rods* for dim light (*scotopic*) vision and *cones* for colour (*photopic*) vision and fine detail. Cones are concentrated at the *fovea* *centralis*, the area of highest visual *acuity*. The visual pigment in rods is *rhodopsin*, which contains *retinal* (a derivative of vitamin A). Deficiency of vitamin A causes *night* *blindness* (nyctalopia).

The *ossicular* chain (*malleus*, *incus*, *stapes*) transmits sound from the *tympanic* membrane to the *oval* window of the *cochlea*. The *organ* of *Corti* contains inner and outer *hair* cells that transduce mechanical vibrations into nerve impulses. The *tonotopic* organisation means high frequencies are detected at the *base* and low frequencies at the *apex*. *Sensorineural* hearing loss results from damage to the cochlea or *auditory* nerve.

The *semicircular* canals detect *angular* acceleration, while the *utricle* and *saccule* detect *linear* acceleration. *Taste* from the anterior two-thirds of the tongue travels via the *facial* nerve (CN VII), and from the posterior one-third via the *glossopharyngeal* nerve (CN IX). *Olfactory* receptors are *bipolar* neurons in the nasal mucosa.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Revisit the terms you missed.
Excellent! You identified the key special senses terms accurately.
